question: What is a function 'parameter' in Python?

options.A: A special tool used to delete a broken function
options.B: An input value or option passed into a function to change how it behaves
options.C: The closing line at the very bottom of a loop
options.D: A variable that can only store negative numbers

question:
Look at this function definition:

def say_hello(name):
    print("Hello", name)

What do you need to type to call this function and pass the name 'Wall-E' into it?

Look at this code:

def drive(speed, distance):
    print("Speed:", speed)

drive(50, 100)

What number will be assigned to the 'speed' variable when the function runs?
Why do programmers add parameters to functions instead of creating multiple different functions?

options.A: Because it makes one function universal and reusable for many different situations
options.B: Because functions with parameters run automatically without being called
options.C: Because parameters protect the code from infinite loops
options.D: Because Python doesn't allow you to write more than three functions
